summary: The reproduction starts the vulnerable localhost HTTP server as a victim user, proves the attacker user cannot read the victim's graph files directly, then shows that the attacker can still query and poison the victim-owned graph over 127.0.0.1.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that an attacker user with no direct read access to the victim-owned graph can still read project graph data through GET /query and persist a new mistake node through POST /learn.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned upstream tag v2.0.1, built the reproduction image, started the vulnerable localhost service as a victim user, confirmed that the attacker user could not read the victim-owned .engram/graph.db on disk, then showed that the attacker could still query the victim graph and inject a new mistake node through POST /learn. The run ended with the success signal: "[evohunt] SUCCESS: attacker could query and poison the victim-owned graph over localhost without direct file access".
verification_method: The container creates separate victim and attacker users, restricts the victim repo copy so the attacker cannot read .engram/graph.db directly, then verifies that attacker-owned localhost requests still succeed and that the injected mistake node appears in a follow-up query.
evidence_produced_by_run: The reproduction log shows "[evohunt] verifying attacker cannot read victim graph on disk" followed by successful continuation, proving the filesystem check failed closed for the attacker account.
The reproduction log shows "[evohunt] querying victim graph over localhost as attacker", confirming that unauthenticated GET /query succeeded after the direct-file-access check failed.

The reproduction log shows "[evohunt] poisoning victim graph over localhost as attacker" and "[evohunt] verifying injected node is visible through follow-up query", confirming that POST /learn persisted attacker-controlled graph content.
The run ended with "[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified".

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
